nodes.label: SCFF cheap brain final architecture (v1.1.0, noise-hardened)
nodes.rationale: Adds one thing to v1.0.0: the cheap brain hardened against the noise it will actually meet, with the input-transducer residual named.

nodes.label: NoiseAugContrast (noise-hardened objective)
nodes.rationale: Corrupts ONE of the two InfoNCE views at train time so the composed class direction is noise-invariant, forward-only; a surrogate for a Jacobian/Tikhonov penalty. Generic (broad-iid) smoothing wins over directional-specific because the enemy's axis is unknown/label-free at train time.

nodes.label: Directional retention / A7 noise sensitivity
nodes.rationale: The dangerous enemy is a coherent DIRECTIONAL translation along the class axis that barely rotates any single vector (cos~0.97) yet slides the whole cloud off a fixed readout; per-sample cosine is blind to it, so the true read is retention of the class direction (the spine's sixth bill).

nodes.label: AIHWKit analog-noise model (threat model)
nodes.rationale: Honest behavioral noise model; input/tap/ADC noise dominate the loss, not weight noise, and the dangerous residual is directional. Set the threat model: tap >> weight, enemy = the class-axis residual surviving common-mode auto-zeroing.

nodes.label: Training-with-noise ≈ Tikhonov (Bishop 1995)
nodes.rationale: First-order equivalence between injecting input noise and a Jacobian/Lipschitz penalty; why the noise-augmented view works and why generic smoothing is the right lever.

nodes.label: Noise2Noise (Door B)
nodes.rationale: A clean target is recoverable from only noisy observations when the corruption shares the clean conditional expectation; the theory behind Door B — a class direction forming from an all-noisy stream (held, ratio 0.93).

nodes.label: LP-FT (head preserves not manufactures robustness)
nodes.rationale: A trained head preserves but cannot manufacture backbone robustness; the ordering argument that put noise-hardening in SCFF (before the namer) rather than the readout.
